mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods with rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production confirms synchronized demand and supply expansion, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and INDPRO yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in the index of industrial production following a contraction phase indicates supply chain normalization and easing input cost pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of post-contraction rebound periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 7%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 8.0, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and potential demand destruction, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 8.0, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all high-inflation sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in >58% of observations, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and credit-sensitive sectors, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, with positive 60-day outcomes occurring in >60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid quarterly GDP growth combined with CPI levels above 290 signals overheating supply constraints and imminent policy tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in >62% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when tested on out-of-sample data.
